1356	/-- **Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4 substrate helper.**
1357	
1358	A nonzero rational function `f : K(C)` on a smooth proper geometrically
1359	irreducible curve `C / k̄` (with `k̄` algebraically closed and `C`
1360	integral) whose order vanishes at every prime divisor is the image of a
1361	constant under `algebraMap kbar K(C)`:
1362	```
1363	(∀ Q : C.PrimeDivisor, ord_Q(f) = 0)  →  ∃ c : kbar, f = algebraMap kbar K(C) c.
1364	```
1365	
1366	This is the classical Hartshorne I.3.4 statement (`Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = k̄` on a
1367	complete geom-irreducible variety over an algebraically closed field)
1368	combined with the "no zeros / no poles ⟹ globally regular" form of
1369	Stacks 02P0. **Mathlib gap** as of snapshot `b80f227`: Mathlib does not
1370	ship `Scheme.functionField_const_of_proper_geometricallyIrreducible` or
1371	equivalent. Closure requires two ingredients:
1372	
1373	1. **Algebraic Hartogs on a normal Noetherian scheme**: a rational function
1374	   regular at every codim-1 point extends to a global section of the
1375	   structure sheaf (Stacks tag `0BCK`). On a smooth curve `C`, every prime
1376	   divisor is a codim-1 point.
1377	2. **Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = k̄ for proper geom-irreducible curves over alg-closed k̄**
1378	   (Hartshorne I.3.4): a proper geom-irreducible reduced variety over an
1379	   alg-closed field has its global sections of the structure sheaf coinciding
1380	   with the base field, via the finite-dimensionality of Γ over `k̄`
1381	   (cohomological argument, Hartshorne III.5.2) plus algebraic closure
1382	   forcing all elements to be roots of polynomials, hence in `k̄`.
1383	
1384	Both ingredients are project-bespoke Mathlib gaps. The current iter leaves
1385	this as a single typed sorry; the named-helper extraction makes the
1386	upstream dependency explicit for future iterations.
1387	
1388	iter-196 Lane A: extraction of the inline `hf_const` sorry from
1389	`exists_nonconstant_rational_from_dim_eq_two`. -/

1391	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
1392	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
1393	    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
1394	    (_hord : ∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
1395	        Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f = 0) :
1396	    ∃ c : kbar, f = algebraMap kbar C.left.functionField c := by
1397	  -- Stacks 02P0 / Hartshorne I.3.4: two-step closure as documented above.
1398	  --
1399	  -- Step (i): "algebraic Hartogs" extension to a global section. The
1400	  -- hypothesis `_hord : ∀ Q, ord_Q(f) = 0` says `f` has no zeros and no
1401	  -- poles at any prime divisor. On a smooth curve `C / k̄`, every codim-1
1402	  -- point is a prime divisor (the `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` instance
1403	  -- ensures the DVR stalks at those points and identifies `ord_Q` with
1404	  -- the standard valuation). Algebraic Hartogs (Stacks 0BCK; on a
1405	  -- normal Noetherian scheme, `Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = ⋂_{Q codim 1} 𝒪_{C, Q}`)
1406	  -- then exhibits `f` as a global section of `𝒪_C`.
1407	  --
1408	  -- Step (ii): `Γ(C, 𝒪_C) = k̄`. On a proper geom-irreducible reduced
1409	  -- variety over an algebraically closed field, `Γ(C, 𝒪_C) → k̄` (the
1410	  -- structural inclusion of constants) is an isomorphism. Hartshorne
1411	  -- I.3.4: a finite-dim `k̄`-algebra that is connected and reduced is a
1412	  -- product of fields; alg-closedness forces each factor to equal `k̄`;
1413	  -- connectedness forces a single factor; reducedness is automatic for
1414	  -- a `k̄`-algebra over alg-closed `k̄`. The finite-dimensionality of
1415	  -- `Γ(C, 𝒪_C)` over `k̄` is the standard cohomological fact for proper
1416	  -- varieties (Hartshorne III.5.2).
1417	  --
1418	  -- Both steps require Mathlib infrastructure not currently shipped at
1419	  -- snapshot `b80f227`:
1420	  --   * (i) Algebraic Hartogs on a `IsLocallyNoetherian` + normal scheme
1421	  --     with the codim-1 valuation description.
1422	  --   * (ii) `Module.Finite k̄ Γ(C, 𝒪_C)` for `C` proper over `k̄`, plus
1423	  --     the "connected reduced k̄-algebra finite over alg-closed k̄ is k̄"
1424	  --     argument.
1425	  --
1426	  -- Future iterations: when (i) lands as `Scheme.functionField_extend_global`
1427	  -- (or similar) and (ii) lands as
1428	  -- `Scheme.globalSections_eq_field_of_isProper_of_isGeometricallyIrreducible`,
1429	  -- the body becomes a 5-line composition.
1430	  sorry

1433	conditions and nonzero principal divisor, from `dim H⁰(𝒪_C(P)) = 2`.**
1434

1436	linear-algebra + principal-divisor content out of

1438	invocation supplied with the dimension count from `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`).

1440	The helper carries the substantive content independent of the genus
1441	hypothesis (only the dim H⁰ = 2 input is consumed):
1442	
1443	1. **Linear algebra.** The structural inclusion of constants
1444	   `kbar →ₗ[kbar] H⁰(𝒪_C(P))` (`LinearMap.toSpanSingleton` applied to the
1445	   distinguished section `s₁` built from `globalSections_iff_mp` at
1446	   `f = 1` with the trivial order conditions
1447	   `(∀ Q ≠ P, 0 ≤ ord_Q 1 = 0) ∧ (-1 ≤ ord_P 1 = 0)`) has 1-dimensional
1448	   image, so by `hdim : dim H⁰ = 2 > 1` the image is a strict subspace and
1449	   a section `s ∈ H⁰ ∖ kbar · s₁` exists.
1450	2. **Function-field extraction.** Set `f := toFunctionField P hP hPcoh s`,
1451	   non-zero because `s ∉ kbar · s₁` and `toFunctionField` is kbar-linear
1452	   on the constant subspace (the linearity ensures

1457	   II.6.7 statement: a rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with `principal f = 0`
1458	   (no zeros or poles at any prime divisor) is a global unit
1459	   `f ∈ Γ(C, 𝒪_C)^× = k̄^×` on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
1460	   curve over `k̄`. Contrapositive: `f ∉ k̄` ⟹ `principal f ≠ 0`. The
1461	   non-constancy from step 2 supplies the hypothesis.

1622	  have hf_smul_one : f = c • (1 : C.left.functionField) := by
1623	    rw [hfc]; simp [Algebra.smul_def]
1624	  -- Step (c.4): `toFunctionField (c • s₁) = c • toFunctionField s₁ = c • 1 = f`.
1625	  have h_target : Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField
1626	      (C := C) P hP hPcoh (c • s₁) = f := by
1627	    rw [htF_smul c s₁, hs₁, ← hf_smul_one]
1628	  -- Step (c.5): apply injectivity to get `s = c • s₁`.
1629	  have hs_eq : s = c • s₁ := by
1630	    apply toFunctionField_injective (C := C) P hP hPcoh
1631	    rw [← hf_def, ← h_target]
1632	  -- Step (c.6): `s = c • s₁ ∈ span {s₁}`, contradicting `hs_not_const`.
1633	  apply hs_not_const
1634	  rw [hs_eq]
1635	  exact Submodule.smul_mem _ c (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self s₁)
1636	
1637	/-- **Existence of a non-constant rational function regular on `C ∖ {P}`
1638	with at most a simple pole at `P`** (Hartshorne IV §1 Exercise 1.1,
1639	p. 297, the genus-`0` specialisation).
1640	
1641	Concretely, there exists `f ∈ K(C)` such that:
1642	
1643	* `f ≠ 0`;
1644	* `f ∉ k̄` (i.e. `f` is non-constant — for instance, it does not lie
1645	  in the image of the structural inclusion of constants);
1646	* `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose
1647	  generic point is not `P`;
1648	* `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
1649	
1650	iter-177+ body: use `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` to get
1651	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. The image of `1 ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) ≅ k̄`
1652	under the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)` spans a one-dimensional
1653	subspace of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`; choose any section `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`
1654	not in this constant subspace (non-empty because `dim H⁰ = 2 > 1`),
1655	then take `f := lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField P hP s` and
1656	verify the four bullets via `globalSections_iff` applied to `f`
1657	(the forward direction supplies the order conditions from the existence
1658	witness `⟨s, rfl⟩`). The chosen `f` is non-constant because `s` is not
1659	in the constant subspace and `toFunctionField` is `k̄`-linear and
1660	injective on global sections.
1661	
1662	The principal-divisor-non-zero formulation `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
1663	f hf ≠ 0` follows from non-constancy plus the fact that constant
1664	functions have principal divisor zero (the converse — `div(f) = 0`
1665	⇒ `f` constant — uses the integrality of `C` and is the Stacks 02P0
1666	"functions with no zeros and poles are constant" type statement).
1667	
1668	Blueprint reference:
1669	`cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (alias
1670	`cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero` consumed by `RR.4`)
1671	(Hartshorne IV.1 Exercise 1.1, p. 297).
1672	
1673	iter-194 Lane A first body push: the body is now reduced to a one-line
1674	invocation of the named substrate helper
1675	`exists_nonconstant_rational_from_dim_eq_two`, supplied with the dimension
1676	count `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`. The helper carries the substantive
1677	linear-algebra + principal-divisor content (independent of the genus
1678	hypothesis), leaving this corollary's body mechanical. -/
1679	theorem exists_nonconstant_genusZero
1680	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
1681	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
1682	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
1683	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
1684	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
1685	    ∃ (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0),
1686	      (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
1687	          0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
1688	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f ∧
1689	      Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (X := C.left) f hf ≠ 0 :=
1690	  exists_nonconstant_rational_from_dim_eq_two P _hP hPcoh
1691	    (dim_eq_two_of_genusZero P _hP hPcoh _hg)
1692	
1693	end lineBundleAtClosedPoint
1694	
1695	end Scheme
1696	
1697	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity:290`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34	
35	## Status (iter-177 file-skeleton; iter-181 Pin 3 signature refinement)
36	
37	This file was originally the **iter-177 Lane 8** file-skeleton. Each
38	of the three new pinned declarations carries the *intended*
39	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
40	`blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`) with a
41	`sorry` body. Bodies are iter-182+ work after the RR.1/RR.2/RR.3
42	chain matures (in particular `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`
43	and the `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P]))`-non-constant existence corollary of `RR.3`).
44	
45	iter-181 Lane I refined the Pin 3 signature of
46	`iso_of_degree_one`: the abstract function-field-iso existence
47	hypothesis is replaced by the `[Algebra K(C') K(C)]` typeclass binder
48	+ `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` equation, matching what the
49	iter-182+ body needs (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`
50	Decision 2 DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY).
51	
52	The 3 new pinned declarations are:
53	
54	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` — from a
55	   graded `k̄`-algebra hom `k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
56	   irrelevant ideal generates the unit ideal, produce the morphism
57	   `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lifted to
58	   the slice category over `Spec k̄`.
59	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` — for a
60	   non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
61	   proper curve, extract a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` (the
62	   pole divisor `φ^*[∞]`).
63	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one` — a non-constant
64	   morphism between smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves
65	   whose induced function-field map is a `k̄`-algebra iso is an
66	   isomorphism of schemes (Hartshorne I.6.12 specialised to
67	   degree-`1`).
68	
69	The headline pin (Pin 4) is satisfied by the existing AVR.lean target.
70	
71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.
116	
117	## References
118	
119	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`
120	(~535 LOC, 4 pins). Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*,
121	IV §1 Example 1.3.5, p. 297 (the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification);
122	IV §2 opening paragraph, p. 299 (degree of a finite morphism of
123	curves); I §6 Corollary 6.12, p. 45 (function-field ⇔ smooth projective
124	curve equivalence of categories); II §7 Theorem 7.1 (morphisms to
125	`ℙⁿ` via global sections). Stacks Project tag `0AVX` (degree-`1`
126	finite morphism between integral normal schemes is an isomorphism).
127	-/

165	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
205	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
206	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
207	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
208	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
210	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
212	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
213	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
214	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
215	
216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/
218	noncomputable def morphismToP1OfGlobalSections
219	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
220	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
221	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))
222	    (_halg :
223	      f.comp (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) =
224	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
225	    (_hf :
226	      Ideal.map f
227	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
228	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
229	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's
230	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on `projectiveLineBarGrading kbar`.
231	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
232	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
233	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
234	  Over.homMk
235	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
236	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
237	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
238	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
239	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
240	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
241	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
242	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
243	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)
244	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
245	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
246	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
247	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
248	    -- Goal: `X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`.
249	    -- Step 1: convert the `_halg` RingHom equation to a CommRingCat-level equation.
250	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C) =
251	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop := by
252	      rw [← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, _halg, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
253	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom]
254	    -- Step 2: rewrite, distribute `Spec.map`, and apply naturality of `toSpecΓ` plus
255	    -- the `toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso).inv = 𝟙` identity on `Spec (.of kbar)`.
256	    rw [hcc, Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality,
257	      Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
258	
259	/-! ## §2. Pin 2 — the degree of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹` via its pole divisor
260	(`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`)
261	
262	Hartshorne II §6 + IV §2 opening: for a non-constant morphism
263	`φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹` from a smooth proper curve, the function-field extension
264	`k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite, and the degree
265	`[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = deg(φ)` is recovered as the degree of the pullback
266	of any closed point of `ℙ¹` to `C`. Specialising to `Q = ∞`, this
267	identifies `deg(φ)` with the degree of the **pole divisor** of the
268	pulled-back affine coordinate `φ^* u`.
269	
270	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the substantive content is the
271	existence of a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` realising
272	`deg(φ)`; the body in iter-178+ produces this divisor as the pole
273	divisor of `φ^* u` via the Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree
274	identity.
275	
276	This is a *separable* sub-build pin (the lemma is stated independently
277	of the headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` and does not require `g(C) = 0`).
278	-/
279	
280	/-- **A local parameter at the closed point `∞ ∈ ℙ¹_{k̄}`** — a non-zero
281	rational function on `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar)` whose order at `∞` is `1`
282	(in Hartshorne's standard affine convention: `t_∞ = X₀ / X₁`, with `X₁` the
283	coordinate trivialising the chart at `∞`). Returned as a subtype packaging
284	the element together with its non-zero witness, for use in
285	`Hom.poleDivisor` below.
286	
287	**iter-187 substrate gap — single named typed sorry.** The substantive
288	choice (the chart-1 ratio coordinate `X₀ / X₁ ∈
289	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1)`,
290	embedded into `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField`) is iter-188+
291	work. The substrate hooks (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`):
292	- `AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover` chart-`1` open
293	  (`Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean`).
294	- `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` to extract the global ratio function on
295	  the chart, then `germToFunctionField` to embed in `K(ℙ¹)`.
296	- The non-zero witness is automatic from injectivity of the ratio-as-a-
297	  HomogeneousLocalization-Away-element (the ratio `X₀ / X₁` is not the
298	  zero element of the localised graded ring).
299	
300	This helper is the **single substrate gap** carrying the iter-188 unblock
301	for the consumer scaffold `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` Steps 1-5
302	below; the gap is **named, typed, and topologically localised**, not a
303	bare body sorry. -/
304	private noncomputable def localParameterAtInfty (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
305	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left] :
306	    { t : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField // t ≠ 0 } := by
307	  classical
308	  -- The grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`.
309	  let 𝒜 : ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
310	    projectiveLineBarGrading kbar
311	  -- The element `f = X 1` is homogeneous of degree 1.
312	  let f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := MvPolynomial.X 1
313	  have hf : f ∈ 𝒜 1 := MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1
314	  have hf0 : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ 𝒜 (1 • 1) := by
315	    simpa using MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0

317	  let x01 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f :=
318	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk 𝒜 hf 1 (MvPolynomial.X 0) hf0
319	  -- Basic facts about `X 0` and `X 1`.
320	  have hf_ne : f ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 1
321	  have hX0_ne : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 :=
322	    MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 0
323	  -- Step (a): `x01 ≠ 0` in `Away 𝒜 (X 1)`.
324	  have hx01_ne : x01 ≠ 0 := by
325	    intro heq
326	    have hval :
327	        x01.val =
328	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
329	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
330	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero,
331	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk] at hval
332	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk'_apply,
333	        IsLocalization.mk'_eq_zero_iff] at hval
334	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hval
335	    rcases c.2 with ⟨n, hcn⟩
336	    have hcv_ne : (c : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 := by
337	      simp only [← hcn]
338	      exact pow_ne_zero _ hf_ne
339	    exact hX0_ne ((mul_eq_zero.mp hc).resolve_left hcv_ne)
340	  -- Step (b): `Away 𝒜 (X 1)` is nontrivial (needed for `Nonempty (basicOpen f)`).
341	  haveI hL : Nontrivial (Localization.Away f) :=
342	    (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away f)
343	      (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hf_ne)).nontrivial
344	  haveI hAway : Nontrivial (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f) := by
345	    refine ⟨1, 0, ?_⟩
346	    intro heq
347	    have hval :
348	        (1 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val =
349	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
350	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
351	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_one,
352	        HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero] at hval
353	    exact one_ne_zero hval
354	  -- Step (c): the chart-1 affine open `U = D₊(X 1)` is nonempty (via `awayι`).
355	  let U : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.Opens := Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f
356	  haveI hU_nonempty : Nonempty U := by
357	    obtain ⟨p⟩ := (inferInstance :
358	        Nonempty (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f))))
359	    refine ⟨⟨(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).base p, ?_⟩⟩
360	    -- The point lies in the image of `awayι`, which equals `basicOpen 𝒜 f`.
361	    have hrange : (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange = U :=
362	      Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos
363	    rw [show U = (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange from hrange.symm]
364	    exact ⟨p, rfl⟩
365	  -- Step (d): build the section in `Γ(Proj 𝒜, U)`.
366	  let sec : Γ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left, U) :=
367	    (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom.hom x01
368	  -- Step (e): embed in the function field via `germToFunctionField`.
369	  refine ⟨((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.germToFunctionField U).hom sec, ?_⟩
370	  -- Step (f): non-zero via injectivity.
371	  intro ht
372	  -- `germToFunctionField` is injective on integral schemes.
373	  have hinj_germ :=
374	    Scheme.germToFunctionField_injective (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left U
375	  have hsec_zero : sec = 0 := by
376	    apply hinj_germ
377	    rw [map_zero]
378	    exact ht
379	  -- `basicOpenIsoAway` is an iso, hence its `.hom.hom` is injective.
380	  have hiso_inj :
381	      Function.Injective
382	        (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.hom
383	          (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom) :=
384	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _).1
385	  apply hx01_ne
386	  apply hiso_inj
387	  change sec = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom.hom 0
388	  rw [hsec_zero, map_zero]

407	/-- **iter-195 Lane RCI structural lift** — uniformiser witness for the
408	local parameter `(localParameterAtInfty kbar).val` at the prime divisor

411	The rational function `t = X 0 / X 1 ∈ K(ℙ¹)` (`localParameterAtInfty kbar`

414	non-vanishing at every other prime divisor. Equivalently, the zero
415	divisor `(t)_0 = [Y₀]` on `ℙ¹` has degree `1`.

417	This is the uniformiser hypothesis required by
418	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` (the
419	Hartshorne~II.6.9 statement) at the consumer site
420	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank`. iter-195 lifts the iter-194

422	private helper so the substrate need is documented at a single
423	named declaration rather than buried inline in the consumer body.
424	
425	**Closure path (iter-196+; 3-step substantive close).**
426	
427	1. **Witness construction.** `Y₀ : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.PrimeDivisor`
428	   is the closed point of `ℙ¹` corresponding to the homogeneous prime
429	   ideal `(X 0) · 𝒜 ⊂ k̄[X₀, X₁]` (the "point at infinity"). Equivalently,
430	   the image under `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1)` of the unique closed point of
431	   `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X 1))` corresponding to the
432	   maximal ideal generated by `X 0 / X 1`. The `coheight = 1` witness

436	   maximal ideal `(t)` has coheight 1).

439	   the stalk at `Y₀` (as a `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`-stalk) is
440	   isomorphic to the localisation `(Away 𝒜 (X 1))_{(X 0 / X 1)}`, a
441	   DVR with uniformiser `X 0 / X 1`. Then `Ring.ordFrac` of `X 0 / X 1`
442	   at that DVR equals 1.

444	3. **Uniqueness.** `∀ Y, ord_Y(t) > 0 → Y = Y₀`. The function

463	private theorem localParameterAtInfty_uniformiser_witness
464	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
465	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
466	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left] :

468	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y₀ ↑(localParameterAtInfty kbar) = 1 ∧
469	      ∀ Y : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.PrimeDivisor,
470	        Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ↑(localParameterAtInfty kbar) > 0 → Y = Y₀ := by
471	  -- iter-196+ : 3-step substantive close per docstring. The Y₀ witness
472	  -- is the closed point at infinity (the image under `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 1)`
473	  -- of the maximal ideal `(X 0 / X 1)` in `Away 𝒜 (X 1)`); the order

475	  -- `Proj.basicOpenIsoAway` and the `Ring.ordFrac` API on the DVR

478	
479	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
480	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
481	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
482	
483	**iter-190 substantive body (Pin 2 corrective Option (a) — positive part of
484	principal).** The body is the *positive part* of the principal Weil divisor
485	on `C` of the pulled-back local parameter
486	`algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`:
487	
488	  `Hom.poleDivisor φ := positivePart (principal (algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t_∞) _)`
489	
490	Mathematically, `div(φ^* t_∞) = (φ^* t_∞)_0 − (φ^* t_∞)_∞` on `C`, where
491	`(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]` (the zeros of `φ^* t_∞` are exactly the preimages
492	of `∞`, where `t_∞` vanishes on `ℙ¹`) and `(φ^* t_∞)_∞ = φ^*[0]` (the
493	poles are the preimages of `0`). Extracting the positive part of
494	`div(φ^* t_∞)` exactly recovers `φ^*[∞]` on the nose.
495	
496	**Why the positive-part wrap is essential (Pin 2 structural-conflict
497	diagnosis, iter-189 → iter-190 corrective).** The naked principal divisor
498	`principal (algebraMap _ _ t_∞) halg` has degree zero on a complete
499	nonsingular curve (Hartshorne II.6.10 / Stacks 0BE3,
500	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `WeilDivisor.lean`),
501	whereas the RHS `Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)` of
502	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` is positive (= deg φ ≥ 1) at every
503	call site that uses the φ-induced algebra structure. The positive-part
504	wrap restores provability: `degree (positivePart (principal (φ^# t_∞)))
505	= degree (φ^*[∞]) = deg(φ) = [K(C):K(ℙ¹)]` (Hartshorne~II.6.9).
506	
507	The φ-dependence is carried by the `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` instance
508	binder, which at every call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
509	function-field map (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2).
510	Changing the algebra instance changes the divisor; in particular, the
511	unfolding
512	`Hom.poleDivisor φ ⇝ positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t) _)`
513	is visible to definitional unfolding, *not* opaque behind
514	`Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / empty `proof_wanted` placeholders.
515	
516	**Helper budget**: 1 named typed sorry (`localParameterAtInfty`, the
517	local-parameter choice; substrate gap on the chart-`1` ratio coordinate
518	of `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`). The Pin 2 corrective additionally
519	introduces a file-local `WeilDivisor.positivePart` def + named typed-sorry
520	pin `WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_localParameterAtInfty_eq_finrank`
521	above; these are scheduled to migrate to the public
522	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` /
523	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
524	`WeilDivisor.lean` once the parallel iter-190 WeilDivisor prover lands
525	the public versions.
526	
527	Blueprint reference: `def:Hom.poleDivisor` (Hartshorne IV §2 opening +
528	II §6.9; chapter `chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso` §2 "Convention on
529	the pole divisor"); iter-190 plan-phase Pin 2 corrective NOTE on
530	`lem:degree_via_pole_divisor`. -/

532	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
533	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
534	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
535	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
536	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
537	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
538	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
539	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField]
540	    (_φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :
541	    C.left.WeilDivisor :=
542	  -- Pull a chosen local parameter `t_∞ ∈ K(ℙ¹)` back to `K(C)` along the
543	  -- canonical `φ`-induced algebra map, take its principal divisor, then
544	  -- extract the positive part (i.e. the zeros, which on a complete curve
545	  -- coincide with `φ^*[∞]`). The `t_∞ ≠ 0` ⟹ `algebraMap _ _ t_∞ ≠ 0`
546	  -- step is automatic from injectivity of the algebra map
547	  -- `K(ℙ¹) → K(C)` (a ring hom out of a field is injective).
548	  let t := localParameterAtInfty kbar
549	  have halg :
550	      algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
551	          C.left.functionField t.val ≠ 0 := by
552	    intro h
553	    apply t.property
554	    have hinj :
555	        Function.Injective
556	          (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
557	            C.left.functionField) :=
558	      RingHom.injective _
559	    have h0 :
560	        algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
561	            C.left.functionField 0 = 0 :=
562	      RingHom.map_zero _
563	    exact hinj (h.trans h0.symm)
564	  WeilDivisor.positivePart
565	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
566	      (algebraMap (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
567	        C.left.functionField t.val)
568	      halg)

570	/-- **Degree identity for the pole divisor of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹`**
571	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree under
572	finite pullback). The Weil-divisor degree of the pole divisor
573	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ = φ^*[∞]` equals the function-field-extension
574	degree `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)`, where the
575	`K(ℙ¹)`-algebra structure on `K(C)` is the canonical `φ`-induced one (the
576	typeclass binder pins this; per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2
577	convention).
578	
579	**Tier-3 honest sorry (iter-183).** The substantive body is iter-184+ work
580	via the affine-chart-localised `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
581	(Stacks `02RW` / `0AX5`) per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` Decision 2.
582	The strategy: pick an affine open `Spec A ⊂ ℙ¹` containing `∞`; the
583	preimage `Spec B ⊂ C` is finite over `Spec A` (non-constancy + smooth
584	proper curves ⟹ finite); both `A → B` are Dedekind extensions and
585	`Σ_{Q above P} e(Q|P) · f(Q|P) = [Frac B : Frac A] = [K(C) : K(ℙ¹)]`
586	identifies the pole-divisor degree with the function-field degree. The
587	non-constancy hypothesis is consumed in the finiteness reduction.
588	
589	This helper is the **single substantive gap** of
590	`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` after the iter-182 plan-phase
591	signature strengthening: the wrapper body below is sorry-free assembly
592	that reduces to this helper. -/
593	theorem Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank
594	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
595	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
596	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
597	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
598	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
599	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
600	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
601	    (φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)
602	    (_hφ_non_const :
603	      ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar,
604	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
605	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField]
606	    [Module.Finite (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
607	      C.left.functionField] :
608	    Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree (Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ) =
609	      (Module.finrank
610	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
611	        C.left.functionField : ℤ) := by
612	  -- **iter-190 Pin 2 corrective Option (a) closure** + **iter-191 public-pin
613	  -- migration** (per iter-191 plan-phase Lane I `lane-i-positivepart-clash-fix`
614	  -- directive resolving the duplicate fully-qualified-name clash).

617	  -- was `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (algebraMap _ _ t_∞) halg`, whose degree
618	  -- is zero on a complete nonsingular curve (`principal_degree_zero` of
619	  -- `WeilDivisor.lean`) while the RHS `Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)` is positive
620	  -- (= deg φ ≥ 1) at every call site with the φ-induced algebra structure.

622	  -- operator, recovering `φ^*[∞]` on the nose:

624	  --     = Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart (Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal

627	  -- Hartshorne~II.6.9 pin
628	  -- `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` of
629	  -- `WeilDivisor.lean` (iter-191+ body via the affine-chart
630	  -- `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` chain per

635	    C (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val _ ?hLPUnif

637	  --   Scheme.RationalMap.order Y₀ (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val = 1 ∧

647	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
648	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
649	under finite pullback).
650	
651	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
652	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
653	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
654	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
655	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
656	
657	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
658	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
659	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
660	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
661	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
662	
663	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
664	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
665	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
666	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
667	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
668	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
669	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
670	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
671	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
672	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
673	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).
674	
675	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
676	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity).
677	
678	**Signature strengthening (iter-182 Pin 2).** The iter-177 file-skeleton
679	signature output existential `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = (d : ℤ)` was
680	flagged `weakened-wrong` by the iter-181 `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`:
681	the existential is discharged by ANY positive-degree divisor on `C` and
682	does not reference `φ`. iter-182 strengthens the output to pin `D` to
683	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ` (a *specific* divisor depending on `φ`) and
684	pin its degree to `Module.finrank (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
685	C.left.functionField` (the function-field-extension degree `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)]`).
686	The `[Algebra ...]` instance binder pins the intended `Algebra` structure
687	to be the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map (matching the iter-181
688	Pin 3 sig refinement convention). See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`

983	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a non-constant morphism
984	`φ : C ⟶ C'` such that the induced `k̄`-algebra map on function fields
985	`φ^# : K(C') → K(C)` makes `K(C)` a one-dimensional `K(C')`-module
986	(equivalently `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`), the morphism `φ` is an
987	isomorphism of `Over (Spec k̄)`-objects.

990	is encoded as a typeclass binder

992	the equation `Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1`.
993	The intended `Algebra` instance at every call site is the canonical
994	function-field map induced by `φ` (the composite of
995	`Scheme.Hom.stalkMap` at the generic point with the `IsFractionRing`
996	extension to the fraction-field; see Mathlib
997	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField`). The previous signature took
998	an abstract existence wrapper
999	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)` which is strictly
1000	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs: the birational-extension
1001	argument requires the iso to be **induced by `φ` itself**, not an
1002	arbitrary ring iso. See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
1003	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis prompting this refinement.
1004	
1005	iter-182+ body: invoke the scheme-theoretic argument of Hartshorne's
1006	Corollary I.6.12 (Stacks `0AVX`) — equivalently
1007	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
1008	(see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Steps 1–4):
1009	
1010	1. Reduce to `φ` finite (proper + quasi-finite ⟹ finite via
1011	   `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`; quasi-finiteness follows
1012	   from the degree-`1` hypothesis collapsing each fibre to a single
1013	   point at the generic point and then extending by closedness).
1014	2. Apply `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to get
1015	   `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso.
1016	3. Identify `normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of `C'` (smooth
1017	   proper curve over `k̄` ⟹ regular ⟹ normal). The integral
1018	   closure `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is then an equality under the
1019	   `finrank = 1` hypothesis (rank-`1` torsion-free coherent inclusion
1020	   on a Dedekind base is an iso).

1024	pushforward `φ_* O_C` is a coherent `O_{C'}`-module of generic
1025	rank `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`; the inclusion `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is an
1026	iso of coherent rank-`1` sheaves (torsion-free coherent of generic
1027	rank `0` is zero on a Dedekind base), so `φ_* O_C = O_{C'}` and `φ`
1028	is the structure morphism of an iso.

929	iter-194+).
930	
931	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically
932	closed field `kbar`, equipped with a finite `k̄(ℙ¹)`-algebra structure on
933	`K(C)` (the canonical `φ`-induced one for a non-constant morphism
934	`φ : C → ℙ¹`), and a non-zero element `t ∈ K(ℙ¹)` which is a **uniformiser**
935	on `ℙ¹` (its zero divisor has degree `1`, encoded by `hLPUnif`: a unique
936	prime divisor `Y₀` of `ℙ¹` with `order Y₀ t = 1`, and no other prime
937	divisor has positive order), the degree of the positive part of the
938	principal divisor of `algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t` equals the function-field
939	extension degree:
940	\[
941	  \deg\bigl((\mathrm{div}\,(\varphi^{\#}\,t))_0\bigr)
942	  \;=\; [K(C) : k̄(\mathbb{P}^1)] \;=\;
943	   \mathrm{Module.finrank}_{k̄(\mathbb{P}^1)} K(C).
944	\]
945	Equivalently, `(div (φ^# t))_0 = φ^*[Y₀]` and `deg(φ^*[Y₀]) = deg(φ)`
946	(Hartshorne II.6.9 specialised at the divisor `[Y₀] ∈ Div(ℙ¹)`).

965	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
966	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]

8	/-!
9	# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
12	project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
13	Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
14	étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
15	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
16	`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
17	Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).
18	
19	## Status (iter-196 carrier-soundness probe)
20	
21	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive
22	type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
23	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`). The carrier
24	sorry bodies have been isolated to `Prop`-valued typeclass instance constructors
25	(`⟨sorry⟩`) following the iter-196 carrier-soundness probe (Option A
26	`Functor.IsRepresentable` pattern). The carrier definitions themselves are now
27	`noncomputable` extractions via `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choice` from the
28	typeclass existence fields, so consumers that *quantify* over the typeclasses
29	(rather than use the global instances) are kernel-clean.
30	
31	The 6 pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
34	   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
35	   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
37	   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
38	   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
40	   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
41	   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
42	   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
43	   representing quotient `Q`.
44	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
45	   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
46	   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
47	   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
48	   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
49	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
50	   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
51	   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
52	   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
53	
54	## Note on type expressivity
55	
56	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
57	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
58	
59	- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
60	  tautological `Spec k`.
61	- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
62	  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
63	  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
64	- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
65	  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
66	  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
67	  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
68	  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
69	- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
70	  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
71	  `picSharp C |_T`.
72	- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
73	  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
74	  group axioms.
75	
76	The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders
77	for the sibling-chapter functors `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` and `Div_{C/k}`) are
78	carrier defs extracted from `Prop`-valued typeclasses `HasPicSharp` and
79	`HasDivFunctor` using `Classical.choice`. They ship in the
80	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` namespace, marked with docstrings
81	explaining they will be re-exports of the sibling-chapter landing once
82	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` land (forward refs
83	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp` and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor`
84	per their blueprint `\lean{...}` pins). Their bodies collapse to a one-line
85	`:= ...` re-export at that point.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` (640 LOC,
90	5 pins). Source: Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (FGA Explained Ch. 9 §9.4;
91	arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 25–35), specifically:
92	- §3 Def. `dfn:Abel` (the Abel map) + Thm. `th:repDiv` (relative effective
93	  divisors representable by an open subscheme of the Hilbert scheme),
94	- §4 Main Thm. `th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch` (FGA representability),
95	- §4 Lem. `lm:qt` (smooth-proper quotient of étale sheaves).

180	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
181	
182	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
183	`C/k` is the `k`-scheme representing the étale-sheafified relative Picard
184	functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`. It is supplied by Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`
185	(specialised to `S = Spec k`, `X = C` via Cor. `cor:algsch`); the
186	representability theorem itself is the next declaration (`representable`),
187	and `PicScheme` is the witness scheme extracted from it.
188	
189	The scheme is separated, locally of finite type over `k`, and a disjoint
190	union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the abelian-group-scheme
191	structure is the content of `groupSchemeStructure`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/

195	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of a scheme over `Spec k` that represents
196	`picSharp C`. The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying
197	site for `PicScheme`; consumers that quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` as a
198	hypothesis remain kernel-clean. -/
199	class HasPicScheme {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
200	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
201	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
202	  has_pic_scheme : ∃ (X : Over (Spec (.of k))),
203	    Nonempty ((PicScheme.picSharp C).RepresentableBy X)
204	
205	/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically

207

216	iter-177+: the body extracts the representing object from

240	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
241	
242	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
243	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` (an `S`-flat
244	closed subscheme whose ideal is invertible) determines a `T`-point of the
245	Hilbert functor `Hilb_{C/k}(T)` (hence of `Div_{C/k}(T)`), and the dual
246	ideal sheaf `O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` is an invertible sheaf on the
247	relative curve, representing a class in `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)`.
248	
249	The assignment `D ↦ [O_{C ×_k T}(D)]` is functorial in `T` (pullback of an
250	ideal sheaf along `id_C ×_k g : C ×_k T' ⟶ C ×_k T` takes the ideal of `D`
251	to the ideal of `D_{T'}`, since `D` is flat over `T` and hence
252	`D_{T'} = D ×_T T'` is again a relative effective divisor by Kleiman §3
253	Lem. `lm:ctn`).
254	
255	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
256	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
257	
258	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the Abel map `Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
259	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
260	`abelMap`. -/
261	class HasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
262	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
263	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
264	  has_abel_map : Nonempty (divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C)
265	
266	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`, sending a
267	relative effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated
268	invertible sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
269	
270	A natural transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`. The source
271	`divFunctor C` is the relative-divisor functor (the open subfunctor of the
272	Hilbert/Quot scheme cut out by the effective-divisor criterion); the target
273	`picSharp C` is the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor.
274	
275	iter-177+: the body constructs the natural transformation component-wise:
276	for each test `T`, send a relative effective Cartier divisor `D` on
277	`C ×_k T` to the dual of its ideal sheaf, then take the étale-sheafified
278	class. Naturality is the functoriality of the dual-of-ideal-sheaf

280	The body is extracted from `HasAbelMap.has_abel_map` via `Classical.choice`. -/
281	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]

296	/-! ## §3. The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent
297	
298	The structural lemma underlying Step 4 of Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` is the
299	Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma**: given a surjection
300	`α : Z ⟶ P` of étale sheaves with `R := Z ×_P Z` representable, `Z`
301	quasi-projective, and the first projection `R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper,
302	the quotient `P` is representable by a quasi-projective scheme.
303	
304	Kleiman's proof:
305	1. `R ↪ Z ×_k Z` is a flat (from (iv)) and proper monomorphism, hence a
306	   closed immersion by EGA IV 8.11.5.
307	2. By the theory of flat-and-proper effective equivalence relations on
308	   quasi-projective schemes (Altman–Kleiman), there exist a
309	   quasi-projective scheme `Q` and a faithfully flat, projective map
310	   `Z ⟶ Q` with `R = Z ×_Q Z`. The map `Z ⟶ Q` is smooth (its fibers
311	   coincide with those of `R ⟶ Z`, which are smooth).
312	3. In the category of étale sheaves, both `Q` and `P` are coequalisers of
313	   `R ⇒ Z`; uniqueness of the coequaliser gives `Q ≃ P`.
314	
315	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_proper_quotient` (Kleiman §4 Lem.
316	`lm:qt`). -/
317	
318	/-- **The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent of an
319	étale-sheaf surjection.**
320	
321	Let `Z, P : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u` be presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`, and let
322	`α : Z ⟶ P` be a natural transformation. Assume:
323	1. `Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `Y` (encoded by the
324	   `Z.RepresentableBy Y` hypothesis),
325	2. the pullback presheaf `R := Z ×_P Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `R`
326	   (encoded by the `(Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R` hypothesis),
327	3. the first projection `π : R ⟶ Y` (after representation) is smooth and
328	   proper (encoded by the typeclass hypotheses `[Smooth π.left]` and
329	   `[IsProper π.left]`),
330	4. `α` is an étale surjection (encoded as the lift property: every
331	   `T`-point `p ∈ P(T)` lifts to a `T'`-point `z ∈ Z(T')` for some test
332	   morphism `T ⟶ T'`).
333	
334	Then `P` is representable (by a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Q`).
335	
336	iter-177+: the body builds `Q` via the Altman–Kleiman effective quotient
337	of the flat-and-proper equivalence relation `R ↪ Y ×_k Y`, then exhibits
338	the coequaliser-uniqueness in the category of étale sheaves to identify
339	`Q` with the representing object of `P`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton
340	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
341	theorem smoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
342	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
343	    (α : Z ⟶ P)
344	    (Y : Over (Spec (.of k)))
345	    (_hZ : Z.RepresentableBy Y)
346	    (R : Over (Spec (.of k)))
347	    (_hR : (Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R)
348	    (π : R ⟶ Y)

356	/-! ## §4. The FGA representability theorem
357	
358	Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Picard scheme (Kleiman §4 Thm.
359	`th:main`), specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k`,
360	`X = C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve: the étale-sheafified
361	relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` is representable by a `k`-scheme,
362	separated and locally of finite type, which is a disjoint union of open
363	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes. The representing scheme is the **Picard
364	scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of `PicScheme` above.
365	
366	Blueprint reference: `thm:fga_pic_representability` (Kleiman §4 Thm.
367	`th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch`). -/
368	
369	/-- Typeclass asserting that `picSharp C` is representable by `PicScheme C`.
370	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
371	`representable`. -/
372	class PicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
373	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
374	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
375	  has_representable : Nonempty ((picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C))
376	
377	/-- **FGA representability of the Picard scheme.**
378	
379	Let `k` be a field and `C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
380	over `k`. Then the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`
381	is representable by the Picard scheme `PicScheme C`.
382	
383	iter-177+: the body assembles the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof:
384	1. Stratify `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` by Hilbert polynomial into a disjoint open
385	   cover `(P^φ)_φ`.
386	2. For each `φ`, bound twists by `m`-regularity to reduce to `P^{φ_0}_0`.
387	3. Factor through the Abel map `A_{C/k}` of `abelMap` to obtain
388	   `Z := P^{φ_0}_0 ×_{Pic^♯} Div_{C/k}`, a quasi-projective open of
389	   `Div_{C/k}`.
390	4. Apply `smoothProperQuotient` to the projection `Z ⟶ P^{φ_0}_0` to
391	   represent each `P^{φ_0}_0`; reassemble over `φ` for the full
392	   `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
393	
394	The body is extracted from `PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable` via
395	`Classical.choice`. -/
396	noncomputable def representable {k : Type u} [Field k]
397	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
398	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
399	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSharpRepresentable C] :
400	    (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C) :=
401	  Classical.choice (PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable (C := C))
402	
403	/-- Existence instance for `PicSharpRepresentable`. This is the single
404	sorry-carrying site for `representable`. -/
405	noncomputable instance instPicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k]
406	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
407	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
408	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSharpRepresentable C :=
409	  ⟨sorry⟩

411	/-! ## §5. The abelian group-scheme structure
412	
413	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` inherits an abelian group-scheme structure
414	from the tensor product of invertible sheaves on `C ×_k T`. The relative
415	Picard presheaf `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` is already an abelian
416	group at every `T` (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
417	functorially in `T`; this lifts to the étale sheafification (sheafification
418	of an abelian-presheaf is an abelian-sheaf, since it commutes with finite
419	products in the value category), and then to a group-scheme structure on
420	the representing scheme by Yoneda.
421	
422	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_is_group_scheme` (Kleiman §2 Def. `df:Pfs` +
423	§4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
424	
425	/-- Typeclass asserting that `PicScheme C` carries a `GrpObj` structure.
426	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
427	`groupSchemeStructure`. -/
428	class PicSchemeGroupObject {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
429	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
430	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
431	  has_group_object : Nonempty (GrpObj (PicScheme C))
432	
433	/-- **The Picard scheme is a `k`-group scheme.**
434	
435	The Picard scheme `PicScheme C` carries a canonical `GrpObj` structure (a
436	group-object structure in `Over (Spec (.of k))`), obtained by Yoneda
437	transport of the abelian-group structure on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)` (sum:
438	`[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`; inverse: `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`; unit: `[O_{C ×_k T}]`).
439	
440	The full abelian-group-scheme refinement is the conjunction of this `GrpObj`
441	instance with the `IsCommMonObj (PicScheme C)` predicate (commutativity of
442	the multiplication), which holds because tensor product of invertible
443	sheaves is symmetric; `IsCommMonObj` is a separate instance threaded on top
444	of `GrpObj` in iter-177+.
445	
446	iter-177+: the body invokes the Yoneda lemma to transport the natural
447	abelian-group structure on `T ↦ picSharp C |_T` to the multiplication /
448	inverse / unit morphisms on `PicScheme C`; the group axioms hold at the
449	representable level functorially in `T`, hence on the representing scheme
450	by Yoneda. The body is extracted from `PicSchemeGroupObject.has_group_object`
451	via `Classical.choice`. -/
452	noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
453	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
454	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
455	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSchemeGroupObject C] :
456	    GrpObj (PicScheme C) :=
457	  Classical.choice (PicSchemeGroupObject.has_group_object (C := C))
458	
459	/-- Existence instance for `PicSchemeGroupObject`. This is the single
460	sorry-carrying site for `groupSchemeStructure`. -/
461	noncomputable instance instPicSchemeGroupObject {k : Type u} [Field k]
462	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
463	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
464	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSchemeGroupObject C :=
465	  ⟨sorry⟩
466	
467	end PicScheme
468	
469	end Scheme
470

10	/-!
11	# Vanishing of `H¹` for skyscraper sheaves on a curve (RR.2.H¹)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.2.H¹** project-side build of the closed-point
14	skyscraper `H¹`-vanishing identity
15	
16	  `dim_{k̄} H¹(C, skyscraperSheaf P (ModuleCat.of k̄ k̄)) = 0`
17	
18	on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`. The file is
19	the iter-191 Lane H **file-skeleton**: each of the eight pinned
20	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching
21	the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_H1Vanishing.tex`),
22	with `sorry` bodies; the iter-192+ closure follows the classical
23	Hartshorne III.2.5 flasque-vanishing argument.
24	
25	The chapter strategy (Hartshorne III §2): an injective resolution of a
26	flasque sheaf yields a flasque quotient at each stage; the
27	global-sections functor is exact on a flasque-to-flasque-to-flasque
28	short exact sequence; hence the right-derived global-sections functor
29	vanishes on a flasque input in positive degree. We specialise the
30	abstract flasque-vanishing to the closed-point skyscraper sheaf, which
31	is flasque because it is the pushforward, along the closed embedding of
32	a one-point subspace, of the constant sheaf on an irreducible base.
33	
34	## Eight pinned declarations
35	
36	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsFlasque` — predicate on
37	   `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`.
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsFlasque.pushforward` — pushforward of a
39	   flasque sheaf is flasque (Hartshorne II.1, Ex. 1.16(d)).
40	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsFlasque.constant_of_irreducible` —
41	   constant sheaf on an irreducible space is flasque (Hartshorne
42	   II.1, Ex. 1.16(a)).
43	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule_flasque_eq_zero` — flasque sheaves
44	   have zero `HModule` in positive degree (Hartshorne III.2.5).
45	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_eq_pushforward_const` —
46	   skyscraper sheaf is the pushforward of a constant sheaf along the
47	   closed embedding of the closure of the support point.
48	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.closure_isIrreducible` — the
49	   closure of the support point of a `PrimeDivisor` is irreducible.
50	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.skyscraperSheaf_isFlasque` —
51	   closed-point skyscraper sheaf is flasque.
52	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` — the
53	   `RR.2.H¹` headline (`dim_{k̄} H¹(C, k(P)) = 0`), obtained by composing
54	   declarations 4 and 7.
55	
56	The eighth declaration also lives as a `private` typed-`sorry` helper at
57	`AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean`. The `private` modifier
58	mangles the internal name of the RRFormula copy, so the public
59	declaration in this file is the one resolved by the blueprint's
60	`\lean{...}` pin and by downstream consumers (`sync_leanok` keys on the
61	fully-qualified public name).
62	
63	## References
64	
65	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_H1Vanishing.tex`.
66	Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.1, Exercise 1.16
67	(flasque sheaves), Exercise 1.17 (skyscraper sheaves);
68	III.2, Proposition 2.5 (flasque sheaves are acyclic).
69	-/

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31

10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31

103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
105	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
106	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:

108	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
109	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),

111	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
